race.name: Muffin Race
race.tagline: Bozeman's beloved casual classic — ski 25 kilometers through the Bridger Mountains and earn a homemade muffin at the finish.

vitals.aid_stations: One aid station mid-course, muffins at finish

climate.description: Late January in the Bridger Mountains brings cold, dry conditions with reliable snowpack. Temperatures typically -15°C to -5°C. Altitude adds bite to the cold.
challenges: High altitude cold — temperatures can drop below -20°C
Dry, powdery snow can make grooming challenging
Wind exposure on open meadow sections

course.primary: A 25km loop through the Bridger Mountains at Bohart Ranch Cross Country Ski Center. The course winds through subalpine forest with meadow openings offering views of the Bridger Range and Gallatin Valley. Moderate climbing with some fun descents.
course.surface: Machine-groomed Nordic trails at Bohart Ranch
course.grooming: Good quality grooming at Bohart Ranch
features: Homemade muffins at the finish line — the race's signature tradition
Mix of forest trails and open meadow sections

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A charming local race with Montana mountain scenery and beloved muffin tradition. Very small field and minimal organization keep it firmly in community-race territory. The Bridger Mountains setting is beautiful.

history.summary: A grassroots race organized by the Bridger Ski Foundation and held at Bohart Ranch near Bozeman. Known for its homemade muffin tradition at the finish line, it's a beloved community event that draws local skiers for a fun winter outing in the Bridger Mountains.
notable_facts: Named for the homemade muffins served at the finish
Bohart Ranch sits at 6,000+ feet in the Bridger Mountains
One of Montana's most casual and community-driven Nordic events
The Bozeman area has a thriving Nordic skiing community
